channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Palestinian businessman lobbies DC with long-shot Gaza vision
item.description: A Palestinian businessman is making a long-shot bid to lead a new governing body of the Gaza Strip, putting up an estimated $300,000 for lobbying services to influence Washington and the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hamas says there's been 'no progress' on second ceasefire phase in indirect talks with Israel
item.description: A senior Hamas official says no progress has been made in the latest round of talks on the second phase of the ceasefire between Israel and the Palestinian militant group Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy And David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis had peaceful night, is resting after breathing crisis, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, had a peaceful night and is resting after a breathing crisis, the Vatican said Saturday. He has been in the hospital since Feb. 14.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands attend funeral for senior Taliban-linked cleric slain in Pakistan's northwest
item.description: AKORA KHATTAK, Pakistan (AP) — Thousands of mourners attended a funeral Saturday for a Taliban-linked cleric slain in Pakistan’s northwest. Hamidul Haq, the head of Jamia Haqqania seminary, was one of seven people killed in a suicide bombing a day earlier at a mosque inside a seminary compound.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainians rally around Zelenskyy as defender of Ukraine's interests after Oval Office blowout
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Soon after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy left the White House on Friday after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with President Donald Trump, Ukrainians rallied around Zelenskyy as a defender of his country's interests.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kurdish militants declare a ceasefire in a 40-year insurgency in Turkey
item.description: Kurdish militants who have waged a 40-year insurgency in Turkey have declared a ceasefire, just two days after their imprisoned leader called for the group to disarm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukrainians won't stop fighting, even if US pulls support: Veteran
item.description: Carl Larson of Seattle fought on Ukraine’s front lines for six months, an experience he describes as “horrific.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy says ire with Trump began with pro-Putin rhetoric
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy says comments made by Trump criticizing Ukraine have prompted his citizens to question what happened to the "friendship" that existed just weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: NY Republican: Putin is ‘the only one that won’ in Trump-Zelensky meeting
item.description: Rep. Mike Lawler (R-N.Y.) has called for President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky to quickly resolve their disagreements after the explosive White House meeting, which he believes was detrimental to Ukraine's interests.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: World leaders back Zelenskyy following Trump, Vance Oval Office spat
item.description: World leaders pledge support for Ukraine and emphasize Russia is the "aggressor" following the Oval Office spat in which the Trump administration accused President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of being "disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European leaders jump to Zelensky's defense after contentious meeting with Trump
item.description: European leaders jumped to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s defense after his contentious meeting with President Trump in Washington that started with a warm tone but derailed swiftly.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Pope had coughing fit, inhaled vomit and now requires assisted ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered an isolated coughing fit on Friday that resulted in him inhaling vomit and requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying an alarming setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mourners bury one of the last hostages released from Gaza as talks start for ceasefire future
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Mourners in Israel on Friday buried the remains of one of the final hostages released in the first phase of the ceasefire between Hamas militants and Israel, as negotiators discussed a second phase that could end the war in Gaza and see the remaining living captives returned home.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How the Trump, Zelensky meeting went off the rails
item.description: Efforts to negotiate an end to the war in Ukraine were left in tatters following President Trump’s confrontational White House meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, which erupted into one of the most remarkable Oval Office scenes in years.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Getting the Israel-Hamas ceasefire to the next phase will be difficult. Here's why
item.description: Israel and Hamas have begun working to advance their ceasefire agreement in Gaza to the next phase, but it's unclear if they'll get there and, if not, what comes next.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope had bronchial spasm, resulted in him inhaling vomit, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Medvedev cheers Trump for Zelensky's 'proper slap down' in Oval Office
item.description: Top Russian official and former president Dmitry Medvedev celebrated what he called President Trump’s “proper slap down” of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in the Oval Office on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: Russia says it is sending new US ambassador in latest sign of thaw
item.description: Russian officials appointed a new ambassador to the U.S. this week, announcing their intention to send Alexander Darchiev to Washington to help mend the troubled relationship between the two global leaders.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Bodies of 12 Pakistani migrants who died in boat sinking have been repatriated
item.description: PARACHINAR, Pakistan (AP) — The bodies of 12 out of at least 16 Pakistanis who died this month when a boat carrying dozens of Europe-bound migrants sank off Libya's coast have been repatriated to Pakistan, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Female American tourist, 37, goes missing during jet ski ride in Thailand: report
item.description: A 37-year-old American tourist reportedly vanished Thursday in Thailand while jet skiing along Patong Beach near Phuket, prompting a search, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driver in Israel injures nine in suspected ramming attack: police
item.description: A driver in Israel plowed into a bus station, injuring nine people, including one person critically, in what police suspect was a ramming attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis' slight kidney insufficiency has receded as he battles pneumonia, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis slept well and was resting Thursday morning as his doctors say the 88-year-old pontiff has shown further slight improvement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red Cross receives bodies of 4 slain hostages during Israel-Hamas exchange
item.description: The Red Cross and Hamas engaged in an exchange on Thursday, in which four dead hostages were exchanged for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners in Israeli prisons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran can manufacture 6 nuclear weapons with newly enriched uranium, UN Atomic agency report says
item.description: A report by the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) cautioned that the Islamic Republic of Iran is producing a large amount of uranium that could be used in six atomic bombs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy is coming to the White House to sign US-Ukraine critical minerals deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy will visit the White House on Friday to sign a long-sought minerals deal that will closely tie the two countries together for years to come.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, HANNA ARHIROVA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US and Ukraine near an economic deal with mineral rights but no security promise, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals, three senior Ukrainian officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, HANNA ARHIROVA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New search to begin for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370: Reports
item.description: The latest search comes after the Malaysian government in December agreed to accept a second "no find, no fee" proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Former Denver developer shot dead at luxury Belize home in likely 'hit': Police
item.description: Boris Mannsfeld was found shot to death at The Villas at Cocoplum, a luxury residential community on the Caribbean country's southeastern coast, Belizean media outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: Animal rights groups, prosecutors trying to stop sale of exotic animals in Tijuana
item.description: "Recently there was a video on social media of someone walking through a mall with a white tiger on a leash," said Ortiz González.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: US refuses to blame Russia for Ukraine war, splitting with European allies in UN votes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — In a dramatic shift in transatlantic relations under President Donald Trump, the United States split with its European allies by refusing to blame Russia for its invasion of Ukraine in votes on three U.N. resolutions Monday seeking an end to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Western leaders visit Kyiv and pledge military support against Russia on the war's 3rd anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Monday marked the bleakest anniversary yet of its war against the Russia invasion, with the country's forces under severe pressure on the battlefield and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration apparently embracing the Kremlin in a reversal of American policy.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets on Sunday for the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, Hassan Nasrallah, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, ABBY SEWELL and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation
